value.found_quote: mcp | mcp(server/tool), mcp(server/*), or mcp(*) | Matches exact MCP tools or all tools on a specified server (applies equally to local mcpl servers and remote connections). Using mcp(*) matches any tool. | Ask
value.rationale: The page explicitly documents an `mcp` permission action in the "Supported Actions & Matching Rules" table. It describes how the action governs access to MCP tools via target formats like `mcp(server/tool)`, `mcp(server/*)`, or `mcp(*)`, and states it "applies equally to local `mcpl` servers and remote connections" — directly matching the claim about governing agent access to MCP tools across local and remote MCP servers. Configuration examples further reinforce this with entries like `mcp(linter/*)` and `mcp(sql/execute_mutation)`. The permission is presented as a currently active, documented feature (not preview/beta), though Terminal Sandboxing is noted as preview separately.
